findings.description: Ensure image alternative is not repeated as text
findings.evidence: Fix all of the following: Element contains element with alt text that duplicates existing text [

findings.description: Slide images appear alongside HTML text extraction of the same content, creating duplicated reading flow throughout the document.
findings.evidence: PDF page 2 shows the 'Why Need River Basin Plans' slide with bullet points. HTML renders the same bullets as text AND embeds the full slide image immediately below, so a reader sees the content twice. Same pattern repeats on pages 3, 4, 12, 16, 20, and others.

findings.description: Bar chart for Stream Condition Middle Deep River (Node 280) is missing from HTML; only the legend text labels and category names are rendered as bullet points.
findings.evidence: PDF page 15 shows a grouped bar chart with colored bars for different QAA percentage ranges across Unimpaired, 2003, 2030, and 2050 Demands. HTML replaces this with a text bullet list of QAA ranges (< 10% of QAA*, 10-20% of QAA, etc.) and scenario names, losing the visual data entirely.

findings.description: Venn diagram showing project organization relationships is missing from the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF page 19 shows three overlapping circles labeled 'Data Collection / HDR', 'Model Development / HydroLogics', and 'Data, Model Evaluation & Plan Development / NCDWR'. HTML replaces this with a flat bullet-point list, losing the visual representation of the overlapping relationships.

findings.description: Table 4-3 Water Supply Deficits data is duplicated: appears both as an embedded image of the original slide table and as a reconstructed HTML table.
findings.evidence: PDF page 16 shows Table 4-3 once. HTML renders the original slide image containing the table, followed immediately by an HTML table containing the same data rows (Orange-Alamance, Hillsborough, etc.), creating duplicate content in the reading flow.

findings.description: Model Development Tasks slide image appears alongside extracted text, duplicating the same content.
findings.evidence: PDF page 20 shows task list (Tasks 1-5). HTML shows extracted text of the tasks AND partially visible slide image with the same task list below, creating reading order disruption.

findings.description: The Water Supply Deficits table from PDF page 16 is rendered BOTH as an embedded image () AND as a fully structured HTML . The PDF contains only one representation of this table.
findings.evidence:
HTML has immediately followed by …
Table 4-3: Water Supply Demand & Deficits…
containing the identical data.
